questions.q: The European powers imposed their urban planning ideas on the colonized cities, which led to the creation of?
Identify the correct statement regarding the development of colonial cities in India.
questions.answer: Colonial cities in India were designed to serve as administrative and commercial centres, with a focus on European-style architecture and urban planning.

questions.q: Explain the significance of the ' Cantonment Towns' in the context of colonial cities in India. (15 marks)
questions.answer: Cantonment towns were established by the British as separate residential areas for their army personnel. They were designed to be self-sufficient, with their own amenities and facilities. The cantonment towns played a significant role in the development of colonial cities in India, as they helped to establish a strong British presence in the region.

questions.q: Who was the British Governor-General of India responsible for establishing the city of Calcutta as a major colonial city?

The colonial powers sought to impose their own cultural and architectural styles on the colonized cities. Discuss this statement with reference to the development of colonial cities in India. (20 marks)
questions.answer: The colonial powers sought to impose their own cultural and architectural styles on the colonized cities in order to assert their dominance and create a sense of superiority. In India, the British imposed their own architectural styles, such as the Gothic and Classical styles, on the cities they established. This was reflected in the construction of buildings such as the Victoria Memorial in Calcutta and the Secretariat Building in Delhi.

questions.q: The city of Madras was founded by?
Explain the role of the colonial powers in shaping the urban landscape of India during the colonial period. (15 marks)
questions.answer: The colonial powers played a significant role in shaping the urban landscape of India during the colonial period. They established new cities, such as Calcutta and Bombay, and transformed existing cities, such as Delhi and Madras, into major urban centres. The colonial powers also imposed their own urban planning ideas, such as the grid pattern, on the cities they established.

questions.q: The city of Delhi was transformed by the British into a major colonial city. Describe the changes that took place in the city during this period. (20 marks)
questions.answer: The city of Delhi was transformed by the British into a major colonial city during the 19th century. The British established a new administrative centre in the city, which included the Secretariat Building and the High Court. They also established a new commercial centre, which included the Connaught Place and the Imperial Hotel. The British also imposed their own urban planning ideas, such as the grid pattern, on the city.

questions.q: The British established the city of Simla as a summer capital. What was the significance of this city in the context of colonial cities in India?
questions.answer: Simla was a popular summer capital of the British during the colonial period. It was a major tourist destination and a centre for European-style architecture and urban planning.

questions.q: The city of Bombay was transformed by the British into a major colonial city. Describe the changes that took place in the city during this period. (15 marks)
questions.answer: The city of Bombay was transformed by the British into a major colonial city during the 19th century. The British established a new administrative centre in the city, which included the Government House and the High Court. They also established a new commercial centre, which included the Horniman Circle and the Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus.
